mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.5 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output that improves short-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 230 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion that improves corporate earnings forecasts, mechanically lifting S&P 500 prices and generating positive forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 95.0 index threshold signals a contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization that depresses corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
